title: NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.20 requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.20 (Governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.41: NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.41' running on index=training (role=executive|board, course_code, completion_date), nis2_management_roster.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC — LMS CSV ingest)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_management_roster.csv
| join person_id [
    search index=training course_code="NIS2_EXEC" earliest=-730d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_completion by person_id
]
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_completion), round((now()-strptime(last_completion,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0), 9999)
| where days_since > 365 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table person_id, role, last_completion, days_since
description: Article 20 requires members of management bodies to undergo training and to oversee cyber risk. This use case tracks board and executive training completions, including NIS2-specific modules, and flags gaps before annual general meeting cycles.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes training. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain roster of persons legally considered management body; (2) map LMS course codes; (3) alert governance committee on gaps; (4) store certificates in HR system with Splunk holding only dates; (5) complement UC-22.2.20 governance dashboard. Indexes required: `index=training`; Lookups: `nis2_management_roster`, `nis2_management_roster.csv`.
visualization: Table (missing training), Single value (% trained), Bar chart (by role), Timeline (training completions).
knownFalsePositives: Operational noise during large maintenance windows, controlled exercises, or scope changes to critical entity lists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC — LMS CSV ingest).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=training` (role=executive|board, course_code, completion_date), `nis2_management_roster.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain roster of persons legally considered management body; (2) map LMS course codes; (3) alert governance committee on gaps; (4) store certificates in HR system with Splunk holding only dates; (5) complement UC-22.2.20 governance dashboard.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_management_roster.csv
| join person_id [
    search index=training course_code="NIS2_EXEC" earliest=-730d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_completion by person_id
]
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_completion), round((now()-strptime(last_completion,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0), 9999)
| where days_since > 365 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table person_id, role, last_completion, days_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence (Art. 20)** — Article 20 requires members of management bodies to undergo training and to oversee cyber risk. This use case tracks board and executive training completions, including NIS2-specific modules, and flags gaps before annual general meeting cycles.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=training` (role=executive|board, course_code, completion_date), `nis2_management_roster.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC — LMS CSV ingest). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_since > 365 OR isnull(last_completion)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: days_since, last_completion, person_id, role.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing training), Single value (% trained), Bar chart (by role), Timeline (training completions).
grandmaExplanation: We stay on top of management body cybersecurity training evidence — so we can show we run security operations and reporting the way the cyber rules that apply to us require.
